stories.title: Inside the Starmer foreign policy team trying to survive in Trump’s world
stories.summary: Inside the Starmer foreign policy team trying to survive in Trump’s world POLITICO spoke to 23 current and former U.K. government aides and politicians to map the network advising the British PM at a moment of crisis. By DAN BLOOM and ESTHER WEBBER in London Photo-Illustration by Natalia Delgado/POLITICO Keir Starmer is trying to manage Donald Trump yet again. He has a long queue of people to tell him how. As the British prime minister tries to navigate one of the rockiest periods ever for the U

stories.title: Péter Magyar maps out grand bargain to reset Hungary’s ties with Brussels
stories.summary: Péter Magyar maps out grand bargain to reset Hungary’s ties with Brussels Hungary’s new leader is racing against the clock to regain access to billions of euros in EU funds, but must at the same time satisfy many demands from Brussels. By MAX GRIERA, NICHOLAS VINOCUR and GREGORIO SORGI in Budapest Illustration by Natália Delgado/POLITICO Hungary’s Prime Minister-in-waiting Péter Magyar knows exactly what he wants from Brussels, and Brussels knows what it expects from Budapest. This i

stories.title: Don’t read too much into Orbán’s defeat
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz had a spring in his step after Hungary’s Péter Magyar stunningly routed incumbent Prime Minister Viktor Orbán in the country’s parliamentary election on Sunday. It is “a good day,” he told reporters , and “a very clear signal against right-wing populism.” But is Merz right to see Magyar’s stunning victory as a rejection of national conservatism and a rebuff of the global far-right movement? Not quite. As Europe’s centrist politicians are gleeful over the do

stories.title: Viktor Orbán inspired rightwingers across the EU and in Britain. His defeat could represent a turning of the tide | Polly Toynbee
stories.summary: We must hope this vote will be the start of a wider backlash – and send hard-right populism back to the fringes where it belongs The forces of darkness rolled back on Sunday. The mighty combined power of Vladimir Putin’s Russia and Donald Trump’s America were defeated in Hungary, as European liberal democratic values triumphed . The populist-nativist right put their all into keeping Viktor Orbán in power. The US vice-president, JD Vance, mid-war in Iran, took time out to parade his patronage in

stories.title: Tuesday briefing: What needs to be done to tackle child poverty in the UK
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: More than one-fifth of ‘austerity-generation’ British children live in poverty. Our social policy editor talks about the damage done and the way forward The austerity years cast a long shadow over Britain. In the wake of the 2008 financial crisis, a programme of cuts overseen by then-chancellor George Osborne and the work and pensions secretary Iain Duncan Smith gutted parts of the welfare state, limiting the generosity of what is now universal credit, introducing a bedroo

stories.title: Fear of populists is pushing governments to oppose expanding the EU
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― Ursula von der Leyen’s vision of a vastly expanded European Union that includes Ukraine is running into a hard obstacle: Many current members don’t want to talk about it. Fear of giving ammunition to populists, nightmare national referendums on each new country joining, and the collective trauma of dealing with Hungary since it joined in 2004 are all contributing to the reluctance in several EU capitals, according to nine EU diplomats and officials who spoke to POLITICO. No nation has

stories.title: EU relieved to see back of Orbán but Magyar’s Hungary may still present problems
stories.summary: Even if migration policy and lukewarm support for Ukraine will raise tensions, Hungary will now be arguing for its own interests rather than Russia’s What Magyar’s victory means for EU and Hungary In Brussels, the relief was palpable after the defeat of Viktor Orbán, the EU leader who recently declared himself at Vladimir Putin’s service . For the EU, Péter Magyar’s victory was all the sweeter, as voters decisively rejected Orbán’s fear-mongering campaign that sought to portray him in cahoots wi

stories.title: Hungary's Magyar outlines policy in first news conference
stories.summary: Prospective prime minister Peter Magyar has promised to move Budapest back towards the European Union and away from Russia. However, he is likely to continue Viktor Orban's hardline policies on immigration.

stories.title: Florida teenager charged with sexually assaulting and killing stepsister Anna Kepner on cruise ship
stories.summary: Kepner’s body was found concealed under bed in a room she shared with two teens, including 16-year-old stepbrother Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A 16-year-old boy has been charged with murder and aggravated sexual abuse in Florida in the 6 November death of his 18-year-old stepsister on a Carnival cruise ship, the US justice department said Monday. Timothy Hudson was initially charged in February and subsequently indicted on 10 March. But the breadth of the case was not known

stories.title: What next for Hungary as country celebrates Péter Magyar victory over Viktor Orbán?
stories.summary: People in the capital, Budapest, and across Hungary celebrated the victory of the opposition party Tisza, led by Péter Magyar, which has won the parliamentary elections, putting an end to the 16-year rule of the populist Viktor Orbán. The Guardian's Europe reporter Jakub Krupa reports from the Hungarian capital Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power Europe live – latest updates Continue reading...

stories.title: Will Orbán’s defeat in Hungary be a turning point for Europe? – The Latest
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule has come to an end after the opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, won the Hungary election by a landslide. Orbán quickly conceded after what he described as a 'painful but unambiguous' result. His defeat will come as a blow to Donald Trump, who has repeatedly endorsed Orbán and whose vice-president, JD Vance, had travelled to Budapest days before the election. All eyes are now on Magyar to see how Hungary’s relationship with the EU and Nato could change after

stories.title: Orbán’s defeat holds lessons for US: ‘Autocrats may rise, but are not invincible’
stories.summary: Stunning loss of rightwing populist in Hungary carries symbolic significance for opponents of Donald Trump For US Democrats seeking rays of light in the dark landscape of Donald Trump’s authoritarian onslaught, illumination has arrived from the unlikely source of Budapest. Viktor Orbán’s stunning defeat in Hungary’s general election – ending 16 years of unbroken rule for his governing Fidesz party – carries symbolic and psychological significance for American politics out of all proportion to th

stories.title: The Guardian view on Hungary’s election: democracy reclaimed | Editorial
stories.summary: The defeat of Orbánism is a globally significant political moment. But it is, above all, a triumph for the citizens who mobilised to take their country back Prior to his landslide election victory in 2010, which was to lead to 16 unbroken years in power, Viktor Orbán would tell supporters: “We have only to win once, but then properly.” Achieving a so-called supermajority by winning two-thirds of parliamentary seats allowed Mr Orbán to change the constitution, and begin turning Hungary into

stories.title: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
stories.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

stories.title: Poland and South Korea upgrade ties to strategic defence partnership deal
stories.summary: During a visit to Seoul on Monday, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk and South Korean President Lee Jae-myung pledged to strengthen their cooperation, particularly in defence.

stories.title: 'We cannot ask any country to give up its territory,' Magyar says on Ukraine
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Viktor Orbán had largely centred his electoral campaign on Ukraine and Volodymyr Zelenskyy, blaming Kyiv for the Druzhba pipeline blockade to accusations of meddling in Hungarian elections. Will things change with Magyar in power?

stories.title: Wildberger: Einsatz von KI-Systemen wie ChatGPT teils „sehr gefährlich“
stories.summary: Digitalminister Karsten Wildberger warnt vor einem unkritischen Einsatz von ChatGPT und ähnlichen KI-Systemen, insbesondere bei sensiblen Anwendungen wie psychologischer Beratung. „Wenn man das nicht richtig macht, auch nicht richtige Vorgaben macht, kann das sehr gefährlich sein“, sagt Wildberger dem Pro-Newsletter Technologie & KI . Seine Aussagen sind auch in der aktuellen Folge des Podcasts Power & Policy zu hören. Gerade bei komplexen oder sensiblen Themen sieht der Minister klare G

stories.title: Viktor Orbán is gone. What does his fall mean for Europe? Our panel responds
stories.summary: Hungary’s return to democracy will be hard. But the impact of Péter Magyar’s decisive victory could be profound, inside the country and beyond Continue reading...

stories.title: Vance’s bad week: vice-president risks becoming face of two Trump foreign policy failures
stories.summary: Orbán is out in Hungary and talks have failed to end the war in Iran – ill-fated road trip has been setback for Maga aims Shortly before JD Vance’s ill-fated week crisscrossing the world, Donald Trump asked him during a private Easter brunch about how the Iran negotiations were shaping up. “If it doesn’t happen, I’m blaming JD Vance,” Trump said to laughs in the room. “If it does happen, I’m taking full credit.” The joke at Vance’s expense contained an unfortunate nugget of truth: this is not an

stories.title: ‘Now I’m hopeful’: Hungarians welcome the morning after 16 years of Orbán rule
stories.summary: Excitement and disbelief mix on the streets of Budapest after record turnout drives opposition’s landslide victory Péter Magyar vows to pursue those who ‘plundered’ Hungary, after election win In a small plaza facing Budapest’s Nyugati train station, Gabor, 40, confessed that he was very, very hungover. “I partied all night,” he said, laughing. “It was crazy, I thought Fidesz was going to win. Now I’m really hopeful.” Continue reading...

stories.title: End of the Orban era: Hungary and Europe breathe again
stories.summary: Viktor Orban's Fidesz party was swept aside in Hungary's parliamentary election. His defeat is a blow for Putin, Trump and populists the world over. Election winner Peter Magyar is promising major change.

stories.title: EU applauds Hungary’s election result
stories.summary: After Hungary’s opposition ousted Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, hopes rise in Brussels that Budapest may abandon its obstructionist course. What is the EU expecting from Peter Magyar?

stories.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
stories.summary: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.title: Hungarian voters celebrate 'much-needed' change
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by 45-year-old ex-party insider Péter Magyar.

stories.title: A lehallgatott és kiszivárogtatott Lavrov-beszélgetések: Szijjártó és Orbán sem fogta fel, hogy Európa nem játék
stories.summary: Amikor azt látták, hogy a színfalak mögötti figyelmeztetések mit sem érnek, más eszközök kerültek elő, de nem a külföld buktatta meg az Orbán-rezsimet. A miniszterelnök láthatóan nem fogta fel: ha nagypályásokkal focizik, miközben az ellenfél csapatát segíti, akkor a játékostársainál előbb vagy utóbb, de betelik a pohár.

stories.title: Magyar Péter egy két évtizedes rémálom végének és Magyarország ébredésének nevezte a vasárnapi választást
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke közösségi oldalán posztolt, történelmi győzelemnek nevezte a választási eredményt. J. D. Vance, amerikai alelnök a Fox Newsnak adott interjújában azt mondta, budapesti látogatása nem Orbán győzelméről szólt. Magyar Péter szerdán találkozik Sulyok Tamással.

stories.title: Tisza has won. What happens next in Hungary until the new government is formed?
stories.summary: The President must convene the inaugural session of the new parliament by May 12, at which time he will propose a candidate for prime minister. Until then, the government’s scope of action remains unrestricted, and the Fidesz-controlled parliament can still pass any legislation. However, a two-thirds Tisza majority can subsequently repeal these laws.

stories.title: Agresszív technikák tették a világ legkelendőbb terápiájává a forradalmi rákgyógyszert
stories.summary: A forradalmi immunterápiás rákgyógyszer, a Keytruda néhány év alatt a világ legkelendőbb gyógyszere lett, miközben sok országban a betegek számára szinte megfizethetetlen. Egy nemzetközi újságírócsapat, amelynek a Direkt36 is tagja volt, egyéves munkával tárta fel, milyen módszereket alkalmaz a szer amerikai gyártója, hogy magasan tartsa az árát, miközben igyekszik késleltetni az olcsóbb alternatívák piacra jutását.

stories.title: Hungary election: Supporters of Peter Magyar's party celebrate in Budapest
stories.summary: Supporters of Peter Magyar, leader of the pro-European conservative TISZA party, celebrate on the banks on the river Danube in Budapest.

stories.title: Thousands celebrate in Hungary as Péter Magyar ends Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule – video
stories.summary: Thousands flooded the streets as Hungary's opposition leader, Péter Magyar, declared victory after his Tisza party claimed a landslide win over Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party. The victory ends Orbán's 16-year rule and marks a significant shift for Hungary's relationship with the EU and Russia. Orbán was celebrated by conservatives across Europe and the US, but lost favour at home with voters who grew weary of economic stagnation, international isolation and oligarchs amassing wealth. His landslide

stories.title: Magyar Péter elmondta, hogy nézett ki az Orbán Viktorral folytatott telefonbeszélgetése
stories.summary: Kollégánk megkérdezte, hogy nézett ki a vasárnap esti telefonhívás, amiben Orbán Viktor gratulált.

stories.title: Sulyok Tamás szerdán találkozik a parlamentbe jutó pártok vezetőivel
stories.summary: Magyar Péter arra szólította fel Sulyokot, hogy minél hamarabb kérje fel a Tisza Pártot a kormányalakításra, és mondjon le.

stories.title: Az ukrán külügyminiszter azt mondta, Zelenszkij felvenné a kapcsolatot Magyar Péterrel
stories.summary: Andrij Szibiha szerint „helyes, európai jellegű hírek érkeztek” Magyarországról, ez új lehetőségeket nyit az orosz–ukrán kapcsolatokban.

stories.title: Szokatlan hangnemben válaszolt a MTVA, amikor a közmédia lehetséges megszűnéséről kérdeztük
stories.summary: „A közmédia a hatályos jogszabályok alapján köteles kiegyensúlyozott és sokoldalú tájékoztatást nyújtani”, írta az MTVA, miután egyszer sem hívták meg Magyar Pétert a választási kampányban.

stories.title: Az éhezők viadala színésze is Budapest utcáin bulizott a választás után
stories.summary: Josh Hutcherson éppen Magyarországon forgat. Ezért futott bele az Orbán tizenhat éves kormányzásának bukását ünneplő tömegbe a fővárosban.

stories.title: Fantasztikusan öregednek a Magyar Nemzet Tisza Párt és Magyar Péter bukását vizionáló cikkei
stories.summary: Annyit mondunk, hogy az utolsó koporsószöget Magyar Péter karrierjébe egy tucat alkalommal verte bele a kormánylap az elmúlt két évben.

stories.title: Magyar: We want to return Hungary to the fore of Central Europe
stories.summary: At his first post-election victory press briefing, Péter Magyar spoke about the first tasks his government will tackle, as well as how he will approach China, Russia, Ukraine, Hungary's neighbouring countries, and more.

stories.title: Magyar Péter lemondásra szólította fel több állami intézmény vezetőjét, megkérdeztük őket, mit gondolnak erről
stories.summary: Az állami szervek kitérő választ adtak, arra nem reagáltak, hogy lemondanak-e a vezetőik.

stories.title: Megszólalt Orbán Viktor
stories.summary: A Fideszben összehívják az aktivistákat és a jelölteket, április 28-án választmányi gyűlést tartanak majd.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Nem jogállamellenes lépésekkel építjük majd újra a jogállamot
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a választások utáni első nemzetközi sajtótájékoztatóján elmondta, miről beszélne Putyinnal és Trumppal, és miért nem követeli Varga Mihály távozását az MNB éléről.

stories.title: Pócs János szerint a fiatalok azért szavaztak a Tiszára, mert új és buli
stories.summary: Tizenhat év után nem jutott be a parlamentbe, de szerinte semmit sem kellett volna a Fidesznek másként csinálnia. Magyar Pétert viszont emberszámba sem tudja venni.

stories.title: László Róbert: El tudom képzelni, hogy Putyin nagyon nagyot csalódott az európai lerakatában
stories.summary: A Political Capital választási szakértője és Bíró-Nagy András, a Policy Solutions vezetője arról is beszélgettek a Telex élő adásában, hogy van-e jövője Szijjártó Péternek.

stories.title: Vučić Magyar Péternek: Ki a keresztapa? Na, gyerünk, válaszolj! Ki a keresztapa?
stories.summary: Magyar azt mondta, hogy tudja, „ki a keresztapa a magyar–szlovák–szerb barátság mögött”. Erre is reagált a szerb elnök, aki kitart Orbán mellett.

stories.title: Vizsgálatot indított az ügyészség, hogy volt-e politikai indíttatása a Gundalfék elleni büntetőeljárásnak
stories.summary: Jámbor András még a választások előtt kérdezte a legfőbb ügyészt, aki most válaszolt.

stories.title: Hungary: Peter Magyar's election heralds thaw in EU ties
stories.summary: Following the ouster of Russia-friendly Viktor Orban after 16 years, Europe is now waiting to see if his designated successor Peter Magyar can make good on his promises to repair ties with Brussels.

stories.title: Record turnout expected as parliamentary election in Hungary gets under way
stories.summary: Polls opened at 6am across the country, and will close 7pm local time. So far, all figures point to a record turnout.

stories.title: Seismic shift: How 16 years of Hungarian politics changed in one day
stories.summary: The National Election Commission said voter turnout was at a record 77.8%, the highest ever recorded in a Hungarian election.

stories.title: Orbán and Magyar vote in high-stakes Hungarian election watched by EU
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán and Péter Magyar vote in a pivotal Hungarian election with record turnout expected, as the result could reshape Budapest’s role in the EU.

stories.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
stories.summary: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule, Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

stories.title: Hungary: jubilant scenes across Budapest follow Magyar’s victory
stories.summary: Hungary saw large crowds take to the streets on Sunday, 12 April 2026, after Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat, ending 16 years in power.

stories.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
stories.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

stories.title: Hungary election: Orban concedes 'painful' defeat to Magyar
stories.summary: Veteran Prime Minister Viktor Orban has acknowledged the landslide victory of Peter Magyar and his Tisza party in Hungary's parliamentary election. Magyar has promised sweeping reforms.

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
stories.summary: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the communist dictatorship.

stories.title: Palestinian groups launch legal bid to shed light on Australian arms export permits to Israel
stories.summary: Three human rights groups are seeking to compel the release of military export permit documents from Richard Marles Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A trio of Palestinian human rights groups have launched a legal bid to force Australia’s defence minister, Richard Marles, to shed light on whether the government has approved Israel-bound export permits, which could violate international law. The groups – the Palestinian Centre for Human Rights, Al-Haq and Al Mezan Cente

stories.title: Trump deletes post with AI image of himself as Jesus-like figure after outcry
stories.summary: The US president’s conservative, Christian supporters decried the Truth Social post, calling it ‘disgusting’ Less than a year after signing legislation that will pull nearly 12 million Americans off health insurance by gutting Medicaid, Donald Trump posted an AI-generated image of himself to Truth Social on Sunday depicting him as a Jesus-like figure, with divine light emanating from his hands as he heals a stricken man in a hospital bed with a demon from hell floating in the background. The pre

stories.title: US military says it killed two people in another boat strike in eastern Pacific
stories.summary: Dispatch of vessel strike, like most of military’s statements on strikes conducted in area, did not provide evidence The US military said it killed two people in a strike on a vessel in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Monday, claiming the targets were involved in “narco-trafficking operations”. The announcement, like most of the military’s statements on the dozens of strikes it has conducted in the eastern Pacific and Caribbean Sea, did not provide evidence to support its claims that the targets we

stories.title: Chagos Islands treaty is now ‘impossible to agree at political level’, UK minister says
stories.summary: Stephen Doughty says US withdrawal of support means bill cannot complete passage through parliament A treaty over ceding sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius has become “impossible to agree at political level” and the corresponding bill will not complete its passage through parliament, a Foreign Office minister has said. Stephen Doughty told the Commons that the agreement with Mauritius was initially negotiated in close coordination with the US, but Donald Trump’s position “appears to

stories.title: Cultural venues in England to share £130m under Arts Everywhere scheme
stories.summary: Galleries, theatres, museums and libraries to benefit from largest cash injection into the arts for a decade More than 100 cultural venues, museums, and libraries will share £130m extra funding as part of the largest cash injection into the arts for a decade, ministers have announced. The investment forms part of the Arts Everywhere Fund, a £1.5bn package to support cultural infrastructure projects over the course of this parliament, which was announced by the culture secretary, Lisa Nandy, earl

stories.title: I do not fear Trump, says Pope Leo after US president calls him ‘weak’
stories.summary: Leader of Catholic church says he will continue to speak out against war after president’s extraordinary criticism Middle East crisis – live updates Europe live – latest updates Pope Leo said he did not fear the Trump administration and would continue to speak out against war after Donald Trump delivered an extraordinary broadside against him in which he said he did not think the Chicago-born pontiff was “doing a very good job”, while also suggesting he should “stop catering to the radical left”

stories.title: US starts naval blockade of Iranian ports after deadline passes
stories.summary: Iran warns Americans they face higher pump prices due to prohibition imposed on Monday evening Middle East crisis – live updates The US blockade of ships using Iranian ports in the Gulf has come into effect, turning the six-week-old conflict between the US-Israeli coalition and Iran into a test of economic endurance. US Central Command (Centcom) made no formal announcement of the start of the blockade but had said it begin on Monday at 5.30pm Iranian time and would apply to any ships entering or

stories.title: Top US Senate Democrats again try for war powers resolution – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed – our live coverage continues here Circling back to Donald Trump’s coming naval blockade, the US military said it would block all Iranian Gulf ports on Monday at 10am ET on Monday (5.30pm in Iran and 1400 GMT) , effectively seizing control of maritime traffic in the strait of Hormuz. “The blockade will be enforced impartially against vessels of all nations entering or departing Iranian ports and coastal areas, including all Iranian ports on the Arabian Gulf and Gulf of Om

stories.title: Mark Carney secures majority government in Canada after special election win
stories.summary: Carney’s Liberals will now be able to pass legislation without the support of opposition parties – and govern until 2029 The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney, has secured a parliamentary majority for his Liberal government, CBC News reported. The victory will help him push through a legislative agenda he says is needed for an increasingly divided geopolitical world. Three special elections were held on Monday in Ontario and Quebec, with two in districts – known as ridings – that have long vo

stories.title: Searching for Satyrus review – on the hunt for an elusive butterfly and the lepidopterist who named them
stories.summary: Rena Effendi attempts to find the species named after her wayward, womanising father – and a connection to the man she never knew – in this moving documentary Photojournalist Rena Effendi’s father was famous in the world of butterflies; he was a lepidopterist who spent seven years hunting one species. Effendi remarks drily that seven years was longer than Rustam Effendi lasted in any of his four – possibly more – marriages. (When asked for a precise headcount of wives, his old friend answers: “G

stories.title: ‘I don’t just ditch my mates’: new Victorian Labor minister defends John Setka ties amid criticism
stories.summary: Luba Grigorovitch, one of four MPs promoted to Jacinta Allan’s cabinet, says she has ‘no regrets’ over friendship with former CFMEU boss Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Luba Grigorovitch, one of four Victorian Labor MPs promoted to cabinet, says she has “no regrets” about her past friendship with disgraced construction union leader John Setka, despite the opposition labelling her appointment “appalling”. The Vict

stories.title: Australia news live: Hanson claims credit for Taylor’s immigration plans; PM arrives in Brunei on fuel diplomacy visit
stories.summary: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Sydney is getting its first new Roman Catholic Cathedral in more than a century, part of a massive 7.7-hectare integrated precinct in the northern suburb of Waitara. Irish architect Niall McLaughlin, winner of the Royal Institute of British Architects’ Royal Gold Medal, will lead the design. The new cathedral is expected to be a unifying force for more than 250,000 Catholics in more than two dozen parishes in t

stories.title: Brazil’s former spy chief who fled country arrested by ICE agents in US
stories.summary: Alexandre Ramagem fled country after he was sentenced to 16 years for his role in plotting military coup in Brazil When Brazil ’s former president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to nearly 30 years in prison for an attempted coup, six other members of his cabinet were also found guilty and all began serving their sentences – except for one. Days before the verdict, Alexandre Ramagem, Bolsonaro’s former spy chief, fled by car to Guyana and boarded a flight to the United States, where he has remained

stories.title: Iran war: Tehran slams US port blockade
stories.summary: Iran has called the US blockade of its ports a "grave violation" of its sovereignty. US President Donald Trump has said Iranian representatives have asked for a deal following fruitless peace talks. DW has more.

stories.title: Canada: Carney secures majority after special election
stories.summary: The Liberal Party's two by-election wins secured a majority in Canada's parliament, allowing them to pass legislation without opposition support.

stories.title: Trump says US military blockaded Iranian ports to ramp up pressure on Tehran
stories.summary: The US blockade comes in an attempt to economically pressure Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to maritime traffic after its six-week closure caused global oil prices to surge. Roughly 40 vessels have passed through since the start of the ceasefire, down from 100 or more per day before the war.

stories.title: Greek police using masked migrants to forcibly push other migrants back across border
stories.summary: Mercenaries recruited since at least 2020 for illegal migrant ‘pushbacks’ in Greece, BBC told

stories.title: Nigeria Air Force orders investigation after dozens killed in airstrike
stories.summary: There are reports that more than 100 people were killed, although the authorities have not commented.

stories.title: UK sets up Brexit-style crisis team to deal with Iran conflict
stories.summary: LONDON — Keir Starmer is establishing a Brexit-style “Middle East response committee” to overhaul how Britain deals with the Iran conflict. The U.K. prime minister will chair the group for the first time Tuesday in an effort to create a central structure underpinning Britain’s international and domestic response and developing policy, said a U.K. government official not authorized to speak publicly. It will comprise two groups, one ministerial and one at official level, and is

stories.title: Iran tries to cosy up to Europe to increase pressure on US
stories.summary: Regime hopes to capitalise on deepening transatlantic split by briefing previously sidelined European countries In a move designed to increase pressure on the US to make compromises in its conflict with his country, Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi has been briefing European capitals on the nature of the offer Iran had been willing to make about its stockpile of highly enriched uranium and future stewardship of the strait of Hormuz during the weekend talks in Islamabad. After the inconclu

stories.title: MSPs not told about collapse of funding deal for Scottish nature restoration
stories.summary: Exclusive: Ministers accused of trying to keep investment firm’s withdrawal from partnership with NatureScot under wraps A funding deal to raise £100m from private investors for urgently needed nature restoration in Scotland has fallen through without the Scottish parliament being told. The Guardian has learned that Aberdeen, the investment firm, decided to withdraw from a partnership with the agency NatureScot to raise at least £100m for conservation projects from commercial and private investo

stories.title: ‘Huge’ increase in kennelling and vet spending by police after XL bully ban
stories.summary: Data from 22 police forces shows spending has more than tripled since the ban came into force in 2024 Police spending on kennels and veterinary bills in England and Wales has more than tripled since the XL bully ban came into force, with some forces recording an almost 500% spending increase since the new law was enacted in 2024. Data from 22 police forces obtained via freedom of information requests showed police spending had soared from an average of £137,400 per force in 2022-23 to £423,136 i

stories.title: Holidays take a hit as UK cost of living fears and Iran war bite
stories.summary: Consumer spending on travel is down for the first time in five years while card spending edges up in March UK consumers have cut back on travel spending for the first time in five years, as they worry about the rising cost of living amid the Iran war. Overall consumer card spending increased 0.9% year on year in March, down from February’s 1%, according to data from Barclays. Continue reading...

stories.title: Giant echidnas weighing 15kg roamed Victoria – and the evidence was hiding in plain sight
stories.summary: A skull fragment found in a tray of unsorted fossils collected more than a century ago leads to discovery Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A prehistoric fossil, hiding in plain sight in museum storage for more than a century, has revealed that giant echidnas once roamed Victoria. The Owen’s giant echidna, Megalibgwilia owenii , lived during the Pleistocene , a geological epoch that began 2.5m years ago. It grew to

stories.title: Qantas cuts domestic flights and raises fares as travel patterns shift due to Middle East turmoil
stories.summary: Australian airline is benefiting from demand for flights that transit through Asia – but says its jet fuel bill is rising sharply Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Qantas has lifted fares and cut domestic flights amid a surge in travel demand away from airlines that transit through the troubled Middle East. The Australian airline says it has redeployed capacity from its US and domestic network to take advantage of

stories.title: French cement maker convicted of financing terror groups to keep its Syria plant working
stories.summary: Lafarge fined more than €1m and its former boss jailed for paying nearly €5.6m to groups including Islamic State A French court has fined the cement group Lafarge more than €1m (£870,000) and sentenced its former boss to six years in prison for paying protection money to Islamic State and other terror groups to maintain its business in war-torn Syria from 2013 to 2014. The ruling follows a 2022 case in the United States in which the French firm pleaded guilty to conspiring to provide material su

stories.title: Israeli forces fire teargas at schoolchildren holding West Bank sit-in
stories.summary: Incident took place on first day back at school in small village, as settlers blocked pupils’ access Israeli forces have fired teargas at Palestinian schoolchildren who were staging a sit-in in the occupied West Bank after settlers blocked access to their school. The Israeli military said it had dispersed an “unusual gathering”, but did not specify whether its troops had fired teargas at the children on the first day of class since the start of the Iran war. Continue reading...

stories.title: Shabana Mahmood says Southport inquiry report exposed ‘systematic failures across multiple public sector organisations’ – UK politics live
stories.summary: The home secretary made the comments after an inquiry revealed that the system ‘completely failed’ Keir Starmer has confirmed that he wants to stop children being exposed to addictive scrolling features on their phones as part of measures to protect them from social media. The PM is under pressure to implement an Australian-style ban on social media for under-16s, and the government is consulting on whether to go ahead with a full ban, or whether to just impose more specific restrictions. It’s n

stories.title: US judge dismisses Trump's lawsuit against Wall Street Journal
stories.summary: The judge threw out Donald Trump's lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal over a story on the US president's ties to late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.title: US: Fugitive Brazilian ex-spy chief arrested by ICE
stories.summary: Former Brazilian intelligence chief Alexandre Ramagem has been detained by ICE in the United States. Brazil formally requested his extradition in December.

stories.title: Benin's finance minister wins presidential election with 94%
stories.summary: According to provisional results, Romuald Wadagni, the election favorite, won a landslide victory in Benin's presidential election.

stories.title: India news: Ambedkar and the idea of Dalit upliftment
stories.summary: As India marks the 135th birth anniversary of BR Ambedkar, DW's special coverage explores the gap between the promise of Dalit upliftment and the reality today.

stories.title: China: Evergrande property developer founder pleads guilty
stories.summary: China's property developer giant has become its also its most indebted and defaulted on most of its liabilities and payments in 2021. Founder Hui Ka Yan "pleaded guilty and expressed remorse," the court said.

stories.title: Colombia approves cull of Pablo Escobar-legacy wild hippos
stories.summary: Decades after the drug lord brought in the first hippos for his private zoo, their descendants have become a threat to Colombia's ecosytem.

stories.title: Germany's Buchenwald: Remembering Nazi atrocities
stories.summary: Buchenwald was one of the largest concentration camps on German soil. Eighty‑one years ago, it was liberated. The commemoration included moving words — and protests.

stories.title: Iran war: Talks collapse is bad news for ordinary Iranians
stories.summary: The collapse of the US-Iran talks has cast fresh doubt over whether the two sides can bridge the gap dividing them. But whether diplomacy will succeed also depends on a range of challenges inside Iran.

stories.title: Spanish PM's wife charged with corruption after two-year probe
stories.summary: Begoña Gómez is accused of using her relationship with Pedro Sánchez to advance her private career.

stories.title: BBC joins paramedics on duty in Lebanon after Israeli strikes
stories.summary: BBC Middle East correspondent Hugo Bachega joins paramedics on duty in Nabatieh - a city that was once vibrant, but is now abandoned.

stories.title: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
stories.summary: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

stories.title: Democrats were already scrambling in California's governor race. Then Swalwell dropped out
stories.summary: California Democrats were already fretting about the race due to troubling polls and low name recognition

stories.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.summary: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: Hospital at centre of child HIV outbreak caught reusing syringes in undercover filming
stories.summary: Footage shows staff injecting without gloves and reusing syringes, but the hospital boss refuses to acknowledge it is genuine.

stories.title: Can this man broaden the appeal of a South African party seen by some as 'too white'?
stories.summary: The Democratic Alliance's new leader, Geordin Hill-Lewis, admits the party's support is too narrow.

stories.title: Quantum computing: A tech race Europe could win?
stories.summary: With some promising computing companies in the field, could Europe be a leader in quantum tech?

stories.title: ‘I don’t know why they’re coming’: Australians on Harry and Meghan's visit
stories.summary: The BBC spoke to people in Sydney to hear their thoughts on the Duke and Duchess of Sussex’s trip to Australia.

stories.title: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.title: Trump uses McDonald’s Oval Office stunt to tout new US tip tax break
stories.summary: Donald Trump staged a McDonald’s DoorDash delivery to the Oval Office to promote new tax deductions on tips, touting relief of up to $25,000.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV celebrates historic Mass at rain‑soaked Basilica in Algiers
stories.summary: Algeria hosted Pope Leo XIV on Monday 14 April for a Mass at the Basilica of Our Lady of Africa in Algiers, marking the first papal visit to the country.

stories.title: South Korea jails 90-year-old woman for laundering son's drug money
stories.summary: The woman has been sentenced to a year in jail for laundering money for her son who is imprisoned in Cambodia.

stories.title: Spain seeks closer China links amid global tensions
stories.summary: Pedro Sánchez meets Xi Jinping in Beijing on Tuesday 14 April 2026, aiming to strengthen political and trade relations at a time of global tension.

stories.title: Trump hosts Dutch royals at the White House for dinner and overnight stay
stories.summary: King Willem-Alexander and Queen Máxima of the Netherlands were received by US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump at the White House on Monday, 13 April 2026, for a formal dinner and overnight stay as part of their three-day visit to the United States.

stories.title: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV in ongoing feud with Catholic Church leader over Iran war
stories.summary: The US president launches an unprecedented attack on Pope Leo XIV over the pontiff's rejection of the Iran war, accusing him of being "weak on crime" and hurting the Catholic Church.

stories.title: Germany cuts fuel tax as oil prices surge on Iran blockade
stories.summary: Following tense exchanges between Economics Minister Reiche and Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil, the government held talks over the weekend. Will the coalition succeed in easing the burden on citizens, or will it collapse?

stories.title: Union Berlin pushes back on sexist comments aimed at trailblazing coach Marie-Louise Eta
stories.summary: Eta has been named interim coach for the last five games of the season as Union aims to avoid relegation from Germany's Bundesliga.

stories.title: Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades
stories.summary: Organisations warn many more people could be executed in the wake of the anti-government protests which swept Iran earlier this year.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV says he has 'no fear' of Trump administration
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV said he has no intention to debate with Donald Trump after the US president criticised him over his comments on the Iran war. "I have no fear of the Trump administration nor of speaking out loudly about the message of the gospel," the pope told reporters aboard the papal plane.

stories.title: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes
stories.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke is accused of living “a life of luxury” in the UK at properties paid for and refurbished by industry figures.

stories.title: Up to 200 civilians killed in Nigeria after air force 'misfire' on market, sources say
stories.summary: Such misfires are common in Nigeria, where the military often conducts air raids to battle armed groups who control vast forest enclaves.

stories.title: Judge dimisses Trump's lawsuit against WSJ over reporting on ties to Epstein
stories.summary: The judge, who was appointed by former president Barack Obama, gave Trump the opportunity to amend his complaint and refile the suit by 27 April.

stories.title: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Super Typhoon Sinlaku threatens Mariana Islands
stories.summary: Rapidly strengthening storm brings destructive winds, flooding risk and dangerous seas to western Pacific Dangerous super typhoon barreling toward group of remote US islands The Mariana Islands archipelago in the western Pacific, home to the US territories of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands, are bracing for extreme weather early this week as Super Typhoon Sinlaku approaches. The system originated as a cluster of thunderstorms over the seas of Micronesia before strengthening into a tropical

stories.title: And the election winner is … the candidate who can afford Africa’s soaring nomination fees
stories.summary: Presidential elections in Djibouti and Benin at the weekend highlighted how a costly electoral system is reshaping democracy Alexis Mohamed would have loved to stand against his former boss. A longtime adviser to Djibouti’s president, Ismail Omar Guelleh, Mohamed resigned last September, citing democratic regression in the country. But at the election at the weekend, Mohamed was not on the ballot. Now outside the country, he says he cannot return home to file nomination papers or campaign freely

stories.title: What is the new EU bill and could it give UK ministers Henry VIII-type powers?
stories.summary: Legislation would allow government to implement evolving single market rules without full parliamentary scrutiny Ministers in Britain are planning a new bill that would bring into force a food and drink trade deal with the EU but also contain powers enabling the government to “dynamically align” with Europe. It would allow the UK to quickly implement evolving single market rules if it determines it is in the national interest, without having to face full parliamentary scrutiny. Continue reading.

stories.title: Pope Leo visits Algeria in sign of Africa’s growing importance to Catholic church
stories.summary: Pontiff makes first papal visit to country as he starts 11-day tour that will also include stops in Cameroon and Angola Pope Leo XIV has arrived in Algeria for the first papal visit to the country, calling for peace on the opening stop of a tour of Africa that signals the continent’s growing importance to the Catholic church. The 11-day trip, which will include stops in Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea, is the longest by Pope Leo since being elected to the papacy in May last year. Continue

stories.title: Renewed ties with EU needed to boost UK security and economy, says Starmer
stories.summary: PM says lessons must be learned from shocks to cost of living as government plans to align with bloc’s rules by default The economic and security benefits of a closer relationship with the EU are “simply too big to ignore”, Keir Starmer has told parliament as the British government prepares for more rapid alignment with European rules. Updating MPs on the Iran conflict and his visit to the Gulf last week, the prime minister was explicit about what he argued was the need for renewed ties with Eur

stories.title: Colombia to cull up to 80 hippos descended from Pablo Escobar zoo
stories.summary: Dozens of feral pachyderms linked to drug kingpin to be killed because of threat to native species and villagers Colombian officials have authorized a plan to cull dozens of hippos descended from animals brought to the country in the 1980s by Pablo Escobar, after the feral beasts displaced native species and threatened local villagers. The environment minister, Irene Vélez, said the decision was reached because other methods to control their population had been expensive and unsuccessful, includ

stories.title: Bahamas police again question US man over disappearance of wife at sea
stories.summary: Brian Hooker says wife Lynette fell overboard from dinghy but family members have cast doubt on that account Police in the Bahamas on Monday were set to again interview a US man who said his wife fell overboard from their boat. In a statement on Sunday to the Guardian, Brian Hooker’s attorney, Terrel Butler, said: “The police have requested another interview with [Brian Hooker] tomorrow.” Continue reading...

stories.title: World's oldest gorilla celebrates 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
stories.summary: Lady Fatou, known as the "grand dame" of the Berlin Zoo, was certified last year by Guiness as the oldest living gorilla in the world.

stories.title: US-Iran war: Is Pakistan a real peacemaker?
stories.summary: Islamabad has been praised for mediating the conflict between Iran and the US, but with an ongoing war with Kabul and a crackdown on the opposition at home, it needs to do some serious work to improve its stock.

stories.title: US maritime restrictions increase economic pressure on Iran
stories.summary: A US naval blockade will cut off Iran's main source of hard currency by halting most of its oil exports. Will Donald Trump's pressure force Tehran back to negotiations or widen the conflict?

stories.title: UK finds attack on Taylor Swift-themed class 'preventable'
stories.summary: A new report on the stabbing that left little three girls dead in UK's Southport concluded that the killer's parents and state authorities failed to act on his violent behavior.

stories.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, cabin crew say they will strike right after
stories.summary: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

stories.title: Dieselgate: Audi bosses, engineers set for long trial as plea deal fails
stories.summary: Two former Audi bosses and two engineers are facing charges over "Dieselgate," a massive emissions scandal that still haunts Germany's automobile sector. As no plea deals could be reached, a long trial seems likely.

stories.title: Why Africa remains divided over Macky Sall's UN bid
stories.summary: Burundi nominated former Senegal president Macky Sall for UN chief, but Africa's backing for him has fractured. AU procedural disputes and regional politics have scuppered a united endorsement.

stories.title: Trump posts and then deletes Jesus-like AI image of himself
stories.summary: The US president denied looking like Jesus in the image, saying he thought it was himself "as a doctor." Trump did not explain why he deleted the post.

stories.title: German Chancellor Merz the most unpopular head of government in the world, survey shows
stories.summary: Surveys show Friedrich Merz as the most unpopular leader across 24 democracies, far behind Narendra Modi, Lee Jae-myung and Andrej Babiš in approval.

stories.title: Britney Spears goes into rehab after driving under the influence arrest
stories.summary: The singer voluntarily checks herself into a treatment facility, three weeks before her court date.

stories.title: Haiti: at least 30 dead in stampede at UNESCO‑listed Citadelle Laferrière fortress
stories.summary: At least 30 people died on Saturday, 11 April 2026, after a stampede at the Citadelle Laferrière near Milot, a major tourist site in the north of Haiti.

stories.title: “I’m not a fan of Pope Leo”: Trump criticises pontiff over Iran comments
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump criticised Pope Leo XIV following comments on the Iran conflict, calling him “very liberal” and saying he was “not a fan” of the pontiff.

stories.title: US to blockade Iranian ports, risking wider war over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US‑Iran talks in Pakistan have collapsed as Trump announced the blockade of Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz starting on Monday afternoon, stoking war fears and pushing oil prices sharply higher.

stories.title: Celebrities and fans pay tributes to Asha Bhosle
stories.summary: Asha Bhosle died in Mumbai on Sunday after being admitted to the hospital following a heart attack.

stories.title: Cherry Blossom Festival in Berlin attracts many tourists this spring
stories.summary: Each spring, Japanese cherry blossoms across Berlin attract many visitors to the German capital. These vibrant pink and white trees, which bloom for only a few days, can be found scattered throughout parks across the city.

stories.title: Pope in Algeria urges forgiveness and reconciliation on historic visit
stories.summary: Algeria welcomed Pope Leo XIV on Monday, 13 April 2026, marking the first visit by a pontiff to the Muslim-majority country, with a focus on interfaith dialogue and peace.

stories.title: Mourners sing Asha Bhosle hit as iconic Indian singer cremated
stories.summary: The singer, who died at the age of 92, will be cremated with full state honours on Monday.

stories.title: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
stories.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

stories.title: Lebanon: Red Cross teams inspect wreckage after Israeli airstrikes damage ambulances
stories.summary: Lebanon saw further damage on Monday, 13 April 2026, as Lebanese Red Cross volunteers inspected ambulances hit by Israeli strikes in the coastal city of Tyre, while rescue teams cleared debris in the nearby village of Maaroub.

stories.title: Thailand marks Songkran New Year with water fights and travel surge
stories.summary: Thailand marked the start of the Songkran New Year on Monday, 13 April 2026, with large crowds gathering in Bangkok for its traditional water festival.

stories.title: US blockade of Iranian ports comes into effect as Trump threatens to destroy 'attack ships'
stories.summary: In a social media post on Sunday, Trump said his goal was to clear the Strait of Hormuz of mines and reopen it to all shipping, but that Iran must not be allowed to profit from controlling the key cargo waterway.

stories.title: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
stories.summary: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

stories.title: Leo becomes first Pope to visit Algeria at start of major Africa tour
stories.summary: Leo XIV wants the world's attention on a continent vital to the Church's future, an aide says.

stories.title: What is a naval blockade and how would it work in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.title: Iran executed at least 1,639 people in 2025 in 68% increase on year before, NGOs say
stories.summary: Rights groups including Amnesty International say Iran carries out the most executions of any nation worldwide per capita, and the most of any country other than China, for which no reliable data is available.

stories.title: Israel summons Italy's ambassador over comments on 'unacceptable attacks' on Lebanon
stories.summary: Lebanon was pulled into the Middle East war when Iran-backed group Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel on 2 March, days after the opening salvo of US-Israeli strikes killed Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei.

stories.title: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
stories.summary: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

stories.title: US blockade of Iranian ports explained in two minutes
stories.summary: The BBC's security correspondent Frank Gardner explains how the US blockade of Iranian ports will work.

stories.title: US drivers head to Native American lands for cheaper gas
stories.summary: Some of the cheapest fuel in the country can be found on tribal land due to tax exemptions.

stories.title: Germany marks 69th birthday of zoo’s oldest gorilla
stories.summary: Berlin Zoo celebrates Fatou, a 69‑year‑old western lowland gorilla, highlighting how specialised care in captivity can extend the lives of endangered species.

stories.title: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
stories.summary: Beijing rejects the allegation as a "farce", while Manila claims the poison was used to kill fish and deprive troops stationed in the area of food.

stories.title: Hezbollah official says group will not abide by any agreements from Lebanon-Israel talks
stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah have fought multiple wars since the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group was formed in the 1980s as a guerrilla force fighting against Israel’s occupation of southern Lebanon at the time.

stories.summary: A Meta olyan csetbotot fejleszt, amivel a cég munkavállalói bármikor beszélgethetnek a főnökükkel.

stories.summary: Az Iránnal folytatott béketárgyalások szombati kudarca után új módszerrel próbálja elérni, hogy a rezsim mindenki előtt megnyissa a szorost. Egy elemző szerint ez a 22-es csapdája.

stories.summary: A szombaton még 20 perces politikai blokkból hétfőre kevesebb mint öt perc maradt, de van helyette kocogó vaddisznó, cseresznyevirágzás és úszkáló hód.

stories.summary: A nemzeti vagyonvisszaszerzési hivatal vizsgálja majd az ezermilliárdokról szóló koncessziót, amely ellen az EU már eljárást folytat. A drága műszaki megoldások szükségességét is át lehetne gondolni.

stories.title: Miniszterek, szóvivők, államtitkárok – ismert fideszesek, akik elbúcsúznak az Országgyűléstől
stories.summary: Nem lesz már képviselő a veterán politikus, a kormányinfók pozitív híreinek tolmácsolója és a született digitális harcos. Sokan éveken, évtizedeken át koptatták a Parlament padjait, de most nem sikerült újrázniuk.

stories.title: A világűrben jött rá, hogy elfelejtette az adóbevallást
stories.summary: Volt egypár perc, amíg az Apollo–13 küldetés legégetőbb problémája az volt, hogy Jim Swigert pilótának indulás előtt nem maradt ideje gatyába rázni a Földön a jövedelemadóját.

stories.summary: Alexandre Ramagem tavaly 16 év börtönt kapott.

stories.title: Felhős idő, zápor jön
stories.summary: A hőmérséklet délután 15 és 21 fok között valószínű.

stories.title: A mérnöki kamara elnöke hosszú posztban kritizálta Lázár minisztériumát
stories.summary: „A díszletek lehullottak, és végre a valóságról beszélhetünk” – tette közzé véleményét fél nappal a választás után Wagner Ernő.

stories.title: Beszakadt a hazai biztonsági üzemanyag-tartalék: a benzin 80, a gázolaj 75 százaléka elfogyott
stories.summary: Úgy fogyasztjuk a tartalékokat, mintha nem lenne holnap: a védett ár miatt bevezetett intézkedés következtében március 31 napjában 47 napi készletet használtunk fel.

stories.title: A Raiffeisen vezető elemzője szerint a költségvetés nagy kihívás lesz az új kormánynak, el kell kezdeni keményen dolgozni
stories.summary: Török Zoltán szerint könnyen lehet, hogy esnek még ki csontvázak a szekrényből, miközben itt a hatalmas első negyedévi államháztartási deficit.

stories.title: Az USA szerint most Iránon a sor, hogy lépjen
stories.summary: Irán felajánlotta, hogy öt évre hajlandó felfüggeszteni nukleáris tevékenységét, de Amerika ragaszkodik a húsz évhez.

stories.title: Hitelminősítő: Komoly gazdasági kihívások várnak a Tisza-kormányra
stories.summary: A Fitch Ratings szerint viszont a kormányváltás után nagyobb az esély, hogy a befagyasztott EU-s pénzek megérkeznek, és arra sem számítanak, hogy az állami intézmények keresztbe tesznek majd az új kormánynak.

stories.title: Trump szerint ő nem Jézus, csak egy orvos a képen, amin kézrátétellel és fénnyel gyógyít
stories.summary: „Orvosként nekem kellene jobbá tennem az embereket, és én jobbá is teszem őket” – mondta Trump.

stories.title: Török Gábor szerint a Fidesz annyi egyéni körzetet fog hozni, amennyit 2014-ben a „totálisan leszerepelt baloldali közös lista”
stories.summary: A politológus szerint a Tisza közel 1 millió győzteskompenzációs szavazatra számíthat, ami 5-6 listás mandátumot jelenthet.

stories.title: Anthony Albanese urges Israel to stop Lebanon attacks that intensified during Middle East ceasefire
stories.summary: PM tells Guardian Australia Hezbollah should cease reprisals and confirms Australia’s military surveillance aircraft will remain in region Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s prime minister, Anthony Albanese, has urged Israel to stop its attacks on Lebanon and raised concern over its intensified military campaign on Beirut and the country’s south after the ceasefire in the Middle East. Albanese also calle

stories.title: Israeli strike kills paramedic, says Lebanese Red Cross – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Our live coverage continues here A post about an hour ago on the Israel Defense Forces Telegram channel claimed that overnight, the IDF “identified a rocket launcher positioned and ready to launch toward the State of Israel in the area of Jouaiyya in southern Lebanon”. Shortly after the identification, the launcher was struck and dismantled in a rapid closure cycle, thwarting the launch before it could be carried out. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
stories.summary: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

stories.title: Merck's Keytruda: A lifesaving drug, a global divide
stories.summary: A cross-border investigation by DW and ICIJ reveals how pricing and patents helped turn a life-saving medicine into one of the world's best selling drugs while leaving many patients struggling to access it.

stories.title: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching 36-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: According to Ukraine's General Staff of the armed forces, by 7am the ceasefire had been violated by Russia 2,299 times, including assaults, shelling and small drone launches. However, Russia has refrained from using long-range drones, missiles or guided bombs.

stories.title: Peru faces protests as election delays fuel doubts over vote integrity
stories.summary: Peru’s presidential election on Sunday, 12 April 2026, was thrown into uncertainty after widespread logistical failures prevented thousands of voters from casting their ballots, delaying the outcome.

stories.title: Bombafenyegetéssel állt bosszút egy amerikai nő az exén
stories.summary: A szakítás másnapján azzal hívta a rendőrséget, hogy a volt párjánál bomba van.

stories.title: Nézőpont Intézet: Realistább volt a Medián felmérése
stories.summary: A Fideszhez közeli intézet kormánypárti előnyt mért, a választások után viszont módszertani önvizsgálatot indított.

stories.title: A kormányváltás miatt egy új epizódra visszatér a Rossz PC Játékok
stories.summary: FreddyD betartja a 2022-es ígéretét.

stories.title: Török Gábor a Medián és a 21 Kutatóközpont méréseiről: Jobban bízhatunk bennük, mint amennyire most tettük, tettem
stories.summary: Szerinte nem halott a szakma.

stories.title: A települések 99,7 százalékában gyengült a Fidesz
stories.summary: A közel 3200-ból mindössze 11 olyan település volt, ahol listán jobb eredményt ért el a Fidesz–KDNP, mint négy éve.

stories.title: Tisza-parttá vált a Balaton-part a választáson
stories.summary: A Balaton utóbbi nyakló nélküli beépítése is szerepet játszhatott abban, hogy a tóparti településeken alig találni olyat, amit a Fidesz nyert.

stories.title: Szabad választás, egyenlőtlen feltételek, elfogult közmédia – így látták az EBESZ megfigyelői a választást
stories.summary: A nemzetközi megfigyelők bírálták a Fidesz és az állam közötti határok elmosódását, de elismerték a választási szervek szakszerű munkáját.

stories.title: Elérhetetlenné vált Mészáros Lőrinc cégcsoportjának honlapja, hivatalosan egy karbantartás miatt
stories.summary: Korábban 504-es hiba fogadta a látogatókat, azóta azt oldal azzal frissült, hogy karbantartás alatt áll, de hamarosan újra várják az embereket.

stories.title: Fellobogóztatta a várost Pécs ellenzéki polgármestere, tekintettel a választás eredményeire
stories.summary: Hiszünk abban, hogy ezek a jelképek nem megosztanak, hanem összekötnek bennünket, közölte Péterffy Attila polgármester.

stories.title: Stánicz Péter: Nem számítok arra, hogy a köztársasági elnökünk visszaélne a jogköreivel
stories.summary: Az alkotmányjogász szerint Sulyok Tamás be fogja tartani a 30 napos határidőt, és össze fogja hívni az új Országgyűlést. Átmeneti időszakról, alkotmányozó többségről és a köztársasági elnök elmozdításának lehetőségéről is beszélt a Telex adásában.

stories.title: Mentővel karambolozott egy furgon a XIII. kerületben, a mentőautó fel is borult
stories.summary: A Hajdú utca és a Fáy utca kereszteződésénél történt a baleset.

stories.title: Karácsony az elszámoltatásról: Úgy nem lesz ebben az országban béke, hogyha nem teszünk ebben igazságot
stories.summary: A főpolgármester a Telex élő műsorában azt mondta a Tisza kétharmadáról, hogy ez óriási lehetőség, de nyilván van benne kockázat.

stories.title: Róna Dániel szerint még három mandátumot szerezhet meg a Tisza
stories.summary: A 21 Kutatóközpont elemzője arról is beszélt a Telex élő műsorában, hogy a Fideszen belül már bőven a választás előtt nagy vereségre készülhettek.

stories.title: Igazságügyi szakértő rekonstruálta, hogyan zuhanhatott le az autó a Váci úti felüljáróról
stories.summary: Legalább 130 km/órával mehetett az Audi, amikor a vétlen Toyotának ütközött. A közlekedési ügyvédet az ámokfutók elleni jogi lehetőségekről, a pszichológust pedig arról kérdeztük, tisztában vannak-e ezek a sofőrök azzal, hogy mások életét veszélyeztetik.

stories.title: Családi békével ért véget az ország legnagyobb molinóháborúja
stories.summary: A balatonfüredi ház országos hírűvé vált erkélypárosán egy tiszás és egy fideszes üzenet feleselt egymással. A választások után azonban a Tiszát támogató Sándor szerint az a lényeg, hogy a politika ne verjen éket barátok, családtagok közé.

stories.title: Visszaszerzett EU-s pénzek, csökkentett áfa, kisegített kisvállalkozók – milyen gazdaságpolitikát ígért a Tisza?
stories.summary: A Tisza választási programja alapján összeszedtük, milyen gazdaságpolitikára lehet számítani, vagyis milyen ígéreteket lehet számonkérni az új kormányon.

stories.title: Toroczkai László a Meta beavatkozása miatt megóvja a választást
stories.summary: A Mi Hazánk pártelnöke kedden ad be kifogást az esélyegyenlőtlenségre hivatkozva a Nemzeti Választási Bizottsághoz.

stories.title: Boosting women's role in politics, Dutch-style
stories.summary: Some 504 women were elected in recent Dutch local elections, with a "Vote for a Woman" campaign boosting their chances to gather preferential votes. Despite the record figure, women remain underrepresented.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz says fuel tax lowered to ease price surge
stories.summary: Berlin is cutting fuel tax and weapons maker Rheinmetall prepares to build missiles in a new venture. A fisherman triggered a massive search effort before being found drunk and asleep in a car.

stories.title: Trump orders Strait of Hormuz blockade after US-Iran talks fail in Islamabad
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump ordered a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after talks with Iran in Islamabad ended without a deal. The move ramps up pressure as both sides blame each other, while Pakistan urges restraint and the ceasefire hangs in the balance.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV urges immediate ceasefire as Israel-Hezbollah clashes rage
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV called for an immediate ceasefire on Sunday as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues, urging all parties to respect international law and protect civilians.

stories.title: Whale Timmy: Can whale songs still save him? Biologist prepares autopsy
stories.summary: For days now, marine biologists have been assuming that there is no rescue for Timmy the humpback whale stranded in the Bay of Wismar. The Environment Minister of Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania is now talking about a "minimally invasive attempt to mobilise the whale".

stories.title: No clouds on the horizon: France and Portugal strengthen their relationship with Treaty of Porto
stories.summary: The Treaty of Friendship and Cooperation between the two countries entered into force this Sunday, a partnership 'at the service of the French and the Portuguese, and of a Europe that decides for itself'.

stories.title: Trump's threat raises risks and leaves predicaments unchanged
stories.summary: The conflict is now a test of wills - Iran’s capacity to absorb strikes versus Trump’s tolerance for the war's costs.

errors.error: <urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
<urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
